title: Pocket Network Relay
description: Schema describing a Shannon relay request and response as proxied by the PATH gateway. A Relay is the canonical unit of work in Pocket Network — every JSON-RPC call to a service (eth, solana, base, etc.) is metered as one or more relays priced in compute units.

RelayRequest.description: JSON-RPC payload routed through PATH to a Shannon supplier.

id.description: Client-supplied correlation ID echoed in the response.

method.description: JSON-RPC method of the target service (e.g. 'eth_blockNumber', 'eth_getBalance', 'getSlot').

RelayResponse.description: JSON-RPC response returned by the selected supplier.

result.description: Successful result. Shape depends on the service and method.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code surfaced by the supplier.

RelayContext.description: Metadata that the PATH gateway adds when scoring suppliers and routing relays. Exposed in development/debug builds; production responses are returned verbatim from the supplier.

supplier_address.description: Bech32 Shannon address of the supplier that served the relay.

application_address.description: Bech32 Shannon address of the application whose stake paid for the relay.

session_block_height.description: Block height of the active session at the time the relay was served.

compute_units.description: Compute units consumed by this relay (1 CU = $0.000000001 USD post-Shannon).

qos_score.description: Quality-of-Service score (0-1) the gateway assigned to the supplier at relay time.
